extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI won Stage 18 to La Roche-sur-Foron after CARAPAZ attacked wrong-side of a roundabout and was caught late by regrouping teammates.
tactical_patterns: CARAPAZ exploited roundabout geometry to attack opposite side, gaining lengths before HIRSCHI could react to tactical positioning.
INEOS sacrificed KWIATKOWSKI mountain points to HIRSCHI early, banking teammate for late regrouping after CARAPAZ breakaway failed.
BENNETT consistently beats SAGAN in identical sprint conditions, revealing pure speed differential not leadout or positioning advantage.
LANDA vaulted to fifth overall exclusively through YATES abandonment, exposing team depth failure when single GC leader cracks.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: First Tour stage win at 30 proves veteran value for stage hunting when released from pure domestique duties.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Took KOM jersey through aggressive descending risk-taking rather than climbing superiority, reveals multi-terrain calculation.
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: positive: Matched CARAPAZ across all intermediate sprints despite teammate interference, showing KOM threat sustainability beyond single-stage efforts.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Clean speed advantage over SAGAN in identical sprint setups confirms green jersey dominance through watts not tactics.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: Defended white jersey passively while ROGLIČ strengthened, suggesting Saturday TT focus over stage hunting.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Dropped teammate KUSS on final climb while extending GC gap, signaling overcapacity reserves before decisive TT.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Abandonment from fifth overall exposes single-leader team fragility when altitude accumulation breaks climber unexpectedly.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Fifth overall gained through YATES abandonment not performance, reveals opportunistic GC positioning without threatening attacks.
